page: UpCloud 2xCPU-2GB is an entry-level virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz. The configuration allocates 2 shared vCPUs across 2 physical cores with 1 thread per core, paired with 2.0 GB of system memory, resulting in 1.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provided via a bundled 60 GB local SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks indicate that this instance is positioned at the lower end of the compute spectrum. Stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests place the server in the bottom 10%, while PassMark CPU and memory scores register in the weak and poor categories respectively. Memory bandwidth tests show weak performance, with read-only large block speeds at 31.43 GB/sec and write speeds at 19.34 GB/sec. However, the server demonstrates average performance in single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression tasks.

Given the shared CPU allocation and limited 2.0 GB memory capacity, this server is designed for budget-conscious, low-intensity workloads. The resource density makes it highly cost-efficient for basic utility tasks but unsuitable for resource-heavy applications. It is best suited for lightweight workloads such as basic web serving, development environments, simple automation scripts, or small-scale testing where high-sustained CPU performance and large memory footprints are not required.
description: The UpCloud 2xCPU-2GB is a budget-friendly, entry-level virtual server utilizing the KVM hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with an AMD EPYC 7542 processor providing 2 shared vCPUs, 2.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 60 GB local SSD. Performance benchmarks place this instance primarily in the lower tiers, with stress-ng CPU and PassMark memory scores ranking in the bottom 10% to 25%. However, it achieves average throughput in single-threaded compression tasks. Due to its shared CPU allocation and modest memory capacity, this server offers a highly cost-efficient profile for low-intensity operations. It is best suited for lightweight workloads, including basic web serving, development testing, and simple automation scripts.
